description: A training backfill joins feature values recorded after the label, inflating offline metrics.
body: Failure mode: a backfill uses a naive join on entity id that attaches the latest available feature value to a historical label, so future information leaks into past training rows and offline metrics look excellent. Detection signals: a model whose validation metric far exceeds any plausible baseline, and a join audit showing feature timestamps after their label timestamps. Do not deploy on the strength of the inflated metric. Safer fallback: rebuild the training set with a point-in-time as-of join that takes only features strictly before each label time, re-evaluate on a temporal holdout, and treat the previous metric as invalid.
